title: Fully qualified order by column gives "relational query without result"
poc: create table x as select name, 1 as a from sys.tables with data;
create table x as select id, CAST(1 as double) as prob from sys.functions with data;
create table y as select id, CAST(1 as double) as prob from sys.functions with data;

with x1 as ( select id, prob, row_number() OVER(ORDER BY id desc) as r from x)
 select y.*, x1.prob
 from x1, y
 where x1.r <=2
 and x1.id = y.id
 -- order by r;
key_bug_features: Fully qualified order by column in subquery
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), age INT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO users (id, name, age) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30), (2, 'Bob', 25), (3, 'Charlie', NULL);
WITH ranked_users AS (SELECT id, name, age, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY users.age DESC) AS r FROM users) SELECT name, age FROM ranked_users WHERE r <= 2 ORDER BY r;
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO orders (order_id, user_id, amount) VALUES (1, 1, 100.50), (2, 2, NULL), (3, 3, 75.00);
